4.2       Where an application is made without notice to P, the witness statement in support of the application must explain why notice has not been given.


4.3       Subject to paragraph 4.4, the application must be completed in accordance with the relevant provisions of paragraph 3.


4.4       The application must be served in accordance with paragraph 9.


4.5       This paragraph applies to an application for variation or discharge of a DAPO without notice to P as it applies to an application for a DAPO without notice to P, except such an application is to be made using Form DA3.


(Section 34(1) of the 2021 Act enables the court to make a DAPO without notice to P where it is just and convenient to do so.  Where the court makes a DAPO without notice to P, it must provide P with an opportunity to make representations about the order as soon as just and convenient at a hearing (section 34(4).)


## 5. Permission to apply for a DAPO


5.1       An application to the court for permission to apply for a DAPO under section 28(2)(d) of the 2021 Act may be made without notice to P.


5.2       An application for permission must be made using Form DA2 and set out—


(a)        whether the contact details for the applicant are to be kept confidential;


(b)        the reasons for making the application on behalf of the person to be protected; and


(c)        the applicant’s connection with the person to be protected.


5.3       Where the contact details for the applicant are to be kept confidential, the applicant must also complete Form C8 (confidential contact details form).


5.4       The application for permission must be accompanied with the proposed DAPO application, completed in accordance with the relevant provisions of paragraph 3, together with sufficient copies for service on P, the person to be protected and any other person directed by the court.


5.5       Where an application for permission is made without notice to P, it must explain why notice has not been given.


5.6       As soon as practicable after receiving an application under paragraph 5.2, the court must—


(a)        grant the application for permission; or


(b)        fix a date for the hearing of the application for permission.
